artifact_identity.series_role: authoritative next-order handoff after the final repo-structure review checkpoint; downstream of the repo-structure series packets and the pre-relayout decision checkpoint
artifact_identity.replacement_target: later first-slice execution receipt or explicit replacement checkpoint after RMS-001 execution

immediate_post_slice_validation_sequence: confirm prose and machine-readable mirrors still align to live root names, ownership claims, and canonical wrapper precedence
verify that no runtime, entrypoint, manifest, or path-resolution code was touched; if any was, stop and reclassify the work
roll back immediately if ambiguity increased or FAST regressed
explicitly_deferred: RMS-002 and every later migration slice
split-root convergence for schema/schemas and packs/data-packs
any XStack/AIDE extraction or platformization work
why_this_order_is_best: the repo-structure series is complete enough to approve a bounded first execution slice
RMS-001 reduces real drift without destabilizing baseline-critical runtime seams
stopping after RMS-001 preserves the stronger product priority of hardening one canonical repo-local playable baseline
the order prevents the repo from flattening planning, approval, and execution into one over-broad relayout step

follow_on_constraint.after_rms_001_succeeds: do not automatically continue to RMS-002
follow_on_constraint.next_broader_work_remains: canonical playable-baseline hardening and its blockers
follow_on_constraint.later_relayout_reconsideration_gate: only after one repeatable repo-local playable baseline exists with fresh FAST and the agreed smoke slice
